content.query:
-- #CREATE MASTER KEY
-----------------------------

CREATE MASTER KEY ENCRYPTION BY PASSWORD = 'Dataengineer6858@'



-- #CREATE CREDENTIAL
---------------------------------

CREATE DATABASE SCOPED CREDENTIAL azureSynapseCred
WITH
    IDENTITY = 'Managed Identity'


-- #CREATE EXTERNAL DATA SOURCE
--------------------------------------

CREATE EXTERNAL DATA SOURCE raw_external_data_source
WITH
(
    LOCATION = 'https://synapseprivatedl.dfs.core.windows.net/raw/',
    CREDENTIAL = azureSynapseCred
)

-- #OPENROWSET FUNCTION
--------------------------------------

SELECT * FROM 
OPENROWSET(
    BULK 'revenue',
    DATA_SOURCE = 'raw_external_data_source',
    FORMAT = 'CSV',
    HEADER_ROW= TRUE,
    PARSER_VERSION = '2.0'
) AS query01 


-- CREATE EXTERNAL FILE FORMAT
CREATE EXTERNAL FILE FORMAT csv_file_format
WITH
(
    FORMAT_TYPE = DELIMITEDTEXT,
    FORMAT_OPTIONS(
        FIELD_TERMINATOR= ''
       
    )
)


--Create an external file format for PARQUET files.
CREATE EXTERNAL FILE FORMAT parquet_file_format
WITH (
         FORMAT_TYPE = PARQUET,
         DATA_COMPRESSION = 'org.apache.hadoop.io.compress.SnappyCodec'   
    );

-- CREATE EXTERNAL TABLE
--------------------------------

CREATE EXTERNAL TABLE revenue_external_table
(
	Dealer_ID VARCHAR(4000),
	Model_ID VARCHAR(4000),
	Branch_ID VARCHAR(4000),
	Date_ID VARCHAR(4000),
	Units_Sold VARCHAR(4000),
	Revenue VARCHAR(4000)
)
WITH
(
	LOCATION = 'revenue',
	DATA_SOURCE= raw_external_data_source,
	FILE_FORMAT= csv_file_format
)

SELECT * FROM revenue_external_table

-- CREATE CETAS
------------------------

CREATE EXTERNAL TABLE revenue_cetas_file
WITH
(
    LOCATION = 'revenue_cetas',
    DATA_SOURCE = raw_external_data_source,
    FILE_FORMAT = parquet_file_format

)
AS
SELECT * FROM 
OPENROWSET(
    BULK 'revenue',
    DATA_SOURCE = 'raw_external_data_source',
    FORMAT = 'CSV',
    HEADER_ROW= TRUE,
    PARSER_VERSION = '2.0'
) AS query01 



SELECT * FROM revenue_cetas_file

-- CREATING SCENARIO 
-- -- If the location doesn't exist then it will create the external table but it can not read 
-- the data until in the storage account that folder name and inside that any file exists

CREATE EXTERNAL TABLE revenue_table_scenario
(
	Dealer_ID VARCHAR(4000),
	Model_ID VARCHAR(4000),
	Branch_ID VARCHAR(4000),
	Date_ID VARCHAR(4000),
	Units_Sold VARCHAR(4000),
	Revenue VARCHAR(4000)
)
WITH
(
	LOCATION = 'revenue_scenario',
	DATA_SOURCE= raw_external_data_source,
	FILE_FORMAT= csv_file_format
)

SELECT * FROM revenue_table_scenario

--CREATE VIEWS
--------------------------

CREATE VIEW revenue_view
AS
SELECT * FROM 
OPENROWSET(
    BULK 'revenue',
    DATA_SOURCE = 'raw_external_data_source',
    FORMAT = 'CSV',
    HEADER_ROW= TRUE,
    PARSER_VERSION = '2.0'
) AS query01 


SELECT * FROM revenue_view

-- #CREATE MASTER KEY
-----------------------------

CREATE MASTER KEY ENCRYPTION BY PASSWORD = 'Dataengineer6858@'



-- #CREATE CREDENTIAL
---------------------------------

CREATE DATABASE SCOPED CREDENTIAL azureSynapseCred
WITH
    IDENTITY = 'Managed Identity'


-- #CREATE EXTERNAL DATA SOURCE
--------------------------------------

CREATE EXTERNAL DATA SOURCE raw_external_data_source
WITH
(
    LOCATION = 'https://synapseprivatedl.dfs.core.windows.net/raw/',
    CREDENTIAL = azureSynapseCred
)


CREATE EXTERNAL DATA SOURCE raw_external_data_source_abfss
WITH
(
    LOCATION = 'abfss://raw@synapseprivatedl.dfs.core.windows.net/',
    CREDENTIAL = azureSynapseCred
)



--Create an external file format for PARQUET files.
CREATE EXTERNAL FILE FORMAT parquet_file_format
WITH (
         FORMAT_TYPE = PARQUET,
         DATA_COMPRESSION = 'org.apache.hadoop.io.compress.SnappyCodec'   
    );

-- ROUND ROBIN DISTRIBUTION
---------------------------------

CREATE SCHEMA bronze;
GO

CREATE TABLE bronze.round_robin_table(
    id INT,
    name VARCHAR(200),
    age INT
)
WITH
(
    DISTRIBUTION = ROUND_ROBIN
);

INSERT INTO bronze.round_robin_table (id, name, age)
VALUES (1, 'akash', 32);

SELECT * FROM bronze.round_robin_table


--REPLICATE DISTRIBUTION
---------------------------------

CREATE TABLE bronze.replicate_table(
    id INT,
    name VARCHAR(200),
    age INT
)
WITH
(
    DISTRIBUTION = REPLICATE
);


--HASH DISTRIBUTION
---------------------------------

CREATE TABLE bronze.hash_table(
    id INT,
    name VARCHAR(200),
    age INT
)
WITH
(
    DISTRIBUTION = HASH(id)
);

---------------
-----------COPY INTO
----------------------------
--We are creating one table in dedicated sql pool and copying our parquet file from data lake storage account to that created table

CREATE TABLE copy_into_table(
   Dealer_ID VARCHAR(4000),
	Model_ID VARCHAR(4000),
	Branch_ID VARCHAR(4000),
	Date_ID VARCHAR(4000),
	Units_Sold BIGINT,
	Revenue BIGINT
)
WITH
(
    DISTRIBUTION = ROUND_ROBIN
);


--LOADING DATA
---------------------------

COPY INTO copy_into_table
(
	Dealer_ID 1,
	Model_ID 2,
	Branch_ID 3,
	Date_ID 4,
	Units_Sold 5,
	Revenue 6
)
FROM 'https://synapseprivatedl.dfs.core.windows.net/raw/revenue_cetas/697ED7B9-64FD-4C3E-A0A5-33C827F9124B_11_0-1.parquet'
WITH
(
	FILE_TYPE= 'PARQUET',
	CREDENTIAL = (IDENTITY = 'Managed Identity')
)

SELECT TOP 10 * FROM copy_into_table;

-- POLYBASE CTAS
-- we are trying to create an external table of a query. first we will select a file , query it by openrowset,
--  CETAS automatically copy this file to another location and put an external layer on top of that copied file

-- over here what has been done is we created an external table from parquet file inside revenue_cetas folder 
-- and we created one real able, and copied data from external table to real table

CREATE EXTERNAL TABLE revenue_polybase_table
(
    Dealer_ID VARCHAR(4000),
	Model_ID VARCHAR(4000),
	Branch_ID VARCHAR(4000),
	Date_ID VARCHAR(4000),
	Units_Sold BIGINT,
	Revenue BIGINT
)
WITH
(
    Location= 'revenue_cetas',
    DATA_SOURCE= raw_external_data_source_abfss,
    FILE_FORMAT= parquet_file_format
  
)


-- CREATE POLYBASE

CREATE TABLE poly_table
WITH
(
    DISTRIBUTION = ROUND_ROBIN
)
AS
SELECT * FROM revenue_polybase_table




SELECT TOP 10 * FROM poly_table;
